assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md: '## Classification: Code Change Request' with Action: 'Create sub-task (subtask-30001.md)'. report.md Review Comments Summary table row: '| 30001 | ... | Code change request -- wrap updates in transaction | Yes (subtask-30001.md) |'. subtask-30001.md exists with the sub-task content.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: '## Classification: Suggestion'. Reasoning section explicitly identifies suggestive language: '"should also add" -- uses "should" in an advisory sense combined with "also"' and '"would help" -- conditional/suggestive phrasing indicating the reviewer believes this is a good idea rather than a required change' and '"Something like" -- explicitly frames the SQL example as an approximation or proposal'.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md: '## Classification: Nit' with Action: 'No sub-task created. This is a nit-level comment that does not warrant a follow-up task.' report.md table row: '| 30003 | ... | Nit -- rename context message | No |'. No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md: '## Classification: Question' with Action: 'No sub-task created. This is a question requiring author/team clarification, not an actionable code change request.' report.md table row: '| 30004 | ... | Question -- GET behavior for deleted SBOMs | No |'. No subtask-30004.md file exists.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 11), '## Implementation Notes' (line 14), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 19). Only one sub-task was created (subtask-30001.md) and it has all required sections.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section (line 25) followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 26).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section (line 28) with 'Review comment #30001 by reviewer-a on modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs line 60:' followed by the full original comment text: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 output files are documentation/classification/report files: report.md, review-30001.md through review-30004.md, and subtask-30001.md. None are source code files (.rs, .py, .js, etc.). No evidence of code file modification in any output.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments have individual classification files: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md. The report.md Review Comments Summary table lists all four comments (IDs 30001-30004) with their classifications and sub-task status.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md overall result is '### Overall: FAIL -- Changes Requested by Reviewer'. No mention of merging, approving, or auto-merging the PR anywhere in the report. The report explicitly states 'reviewer-a has requested changes' and a sub-task was created for the change request.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test file added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs) |'. The Style/Conventions section further elaborates: '**Test Change Classification**: ADDITIVE. All test changes are in a new test file (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`).'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a full '### Convention Upgrade Evaluation' section that explicitly evaluates convention eligibility. It checks for CONVENTIONS.md content, migration file patterns, and concludes: 'The suggestion is NOT eligible for convention upgrade because: 1. The CONVENTIONS.md contents are not available in the fixture data to verify a matching convention. 2. No other migration files are provided to demonstrate a pattern of index creation alongside column additions. 3. General industry best practices do not substitute for project-specific convention documentation.' report.md Observations also notes: 'the fixture data does not include CONVENTIONS.md content to back a convention upgrade.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md Action: 'No sub-task created. This is a suggestion without convention backing.' The convention upgrade evaluation concludes it is NOT eligible. report.md table: '| 30002 | ... | Suggestion -- add partial index (not upgraded, no convention backing) | No |'. No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Eval Quality | N/A | No eval result reviews detected |'. report.md Style/Conventions section: '**Eval Quality**: N/A. No eval result reviews detected -- no reviews matching the three-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker "## Eval Results", footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals).' All three detection criteria are explicitly mentioned.
